summarization.a: The chart shows the relation between SiH₄/NH₃ ratio and Si content and film thickness per cycle. As the ratio increases, the Si content increases until it reaches a plateau, while the film thickness per cycle decreases.

data_extraction.a:
| SiH₄/NH₃ ratio | Si Contents [at.%] | Film thickness per cycle [nm/cycle] |
|---|---|---|
| 10⁻² | 1 | 0.38 |
| 10⁻¹ | 18 | 0.28 |
| 10⁰ | 23 | 0.19 |
| 10¹ | 23 | 0.01 |

a.question: Were SiH4 and NH3 supplied at the same time?
How do the Si content and film thickness change with the SiH4/NH3 ratio?
a.answer: There is an increase in the Si content until SiH4/NH3 ratio of 1 is reached, where a plateau of 23 at% is achieved. Conversely, the film thickness decreases with increasing Si content and SiH4/NH3 ratio.

a.question: At what SiH4/NH3 ratio is saturation reached?
a.answer: Saturation is reached at a value of 23 at% when the SiH4/NH3 ratio is 1.

a.question: Why does the film thickness change when the Si content increases?
a.answer: The film thickness decreases as the Si content increase, due to a reduction in the number of molecules caused by a SiH4 blocking effect, or NH3 deficiency.
